data.title: Upcoming PCE Data Release May Affect Interest Rate Cuts
data.description: The upcoming release of Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) data is anticipated to influence the pace of interest rate cuts, potentially impacting global financial markets. According to NS3.AI, this development could have significant implications for economic strategies worldwide. In related news, Polymarket has announced plans to extend its fee mechanism to sports markets starting February 18. Furthermore, the hearing for the Qian Zhimin case is scheduled for February 16, marking a notable event during the Spring Festival holiday period.

data.title: Ray Dalio Predicts Global Order Collapse: Implications for Cryptocurrencies and Gold
data.description: Ray Dalio has forecasted a breakdown of the global order established after World War II, which may lead to more frequent major conflicts driven by economic pressures. According to NS3.AI, this potential upheaval could ultimately favor cryptocurrencies due to their ability to resist censorship and asset freezes, despite anticipated short-term market volatility. At present, gold continues to hold its position as the leading safe haven, while Bitcoin faces challenges in recovering amidst rising geopolitical and liquidity risks.

data.title: Koko Networks' Collapse Impacts Investors and Industry
data.description: The recent downfall of Koko Networks, a prominent clean-cooking company, has sent shockwaves through the industry, affecting investors who collectively lost $300 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the broader implications of this collapse on the clean-cooking sector. Koko Networks was once considered a leader in its field, but its sudden failure has raised concerns about the stability and future of similar enterprises. The company's demise underscores the challenges faced by businesses in the clean-cooking industry, which has been striving to provide sustainable solutions to energy needs. As stakeholders assess the impact, the focus remains on understanding the factors that led to this significant financial loss and exploring ways to prevent similar occurrences in the future.

data.title: South Africa's Political Party Faces Internal Pressure Amid Coalition Concerns
data.description: South Africa's second-largest political party is experiencing internal pressure to leave the ruling coalition, according to its outgoing leader. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the leader's warning that such a move could harm the party's growth prospects and be a significant error. The leader emphasized the importance of maintaining the coalition to ensure continued political influence and stability. The party's decision will have implications for its future trajectory and the broader political landscape in South Africa.

data.title: Fractal Analytics Shares Decline in Mumbai Debut Amid Valuation Concerns
data.description: Fractal Analytics experienced a decline in its share price during its initial trading session in Mumbai on Monday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing challenges faced by technology startups in attracting investor interest due to valuation concerns. This development comes as the broader stock market remains subdued, reflecting a cautious investor sentiment towards tech companies.

data.title: Strategy Plans to Convert $6 Billion Debt to Equity in Coming Years
data.description:
Strategy founder Michael Saylor has announced the company's intention to convert its $6 billion convertible debt into equity over the next three to six years. According to ChainCatcher, the company stated on the X platform that it has sufficient assets to fully cover its debt, even if Bitcoin's price falls to $8,000.

Currently, Strategy holds 714,644 BTC, with reserves valued at $49 billion, while its convertible debt stands at approximately $6 billion. The company's average purchase price for Bitcoin is around $76,000. With the current trading price at $68,400, Strategy's investment is currently experiencing a loss of about 10%.

Additionally, Saylor shared a chart on the X platform on Sunday, indicating Strategy's continued acquisition of Bitcoin, suggesting another purchase is imminent. This would mark the company's 12th consecutive week of Bitcoin purchases.

data.title: Upbit will launch Bittensor (TAO) spot trading.
data.description: Upbit will launch spot trading for Bittensor (TAO), offering markets for Korean Won, BTC, and USDT.

data.title: Crypto Influencer Faces Significant Losses Due to Leveraged Positions
data.description: Machi, widely recognized in the crypto community as Machi Big Brother, has experienced substantial financial setbacks due to his leveraged trading strategies. According to NS3.AI, Machi holds considerable long positions on Ethereum (ETH) and HYPE tokens, utilizing 25x leverage on ETH and 10x leverage on HYPE. Despite his optimistic outlook, these high-risk trades have resulted in losses exceeding $27.9 million. The situation underscores the inherent risks associated with leveraged trading in the volatile cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Michael Saylor: Plans to convert $6 billion of convertible debt into equity over 3 to 6 years.
data.description: Strategy founder Michael Saylor revealed that the company plans to convert its $6 billion convertible debt into equity within 3 to 6 years. The company stated in a post on the X platform that even if the price of BTC falls to $8,000, Strategy still has sufficient assets to fully cover its debt. Currently, the company holds 714,644 BTC, with reserves valued at $49 billion, and its convertible debt is approximately $6 billion. Strategy's average Bitcoin purchase price was approximately $76,000; based on the current trading price of $68,400, the company's investment is currently at a loss of about 10%. Furthermore, Saylor posted a chart on the X platform on Sunday showing Strategy's increasing holdings, hinting at further Bitcoin purchases, marking the company's 12th consecutive week of buying. (cointelegraph)

data.title: AI Token VVV Experiences Significant Surge on Base Chain
data.description:
AI token VVV on the Base chain saw a notable increase of over 34.2% within 24 hours, according to BlockBeats. The market capitalization reached a recent peak of $530 million, with the current valuation at $510 million. The 24-hour trading volume was reported at $26.6 million.

BlockBeats advises users to exercise caution when investing in meme coins, as they often lack practical use cases and exhibit significant price volatility.

data.title: Japan's Former Currency Chief Criticizes Bank of Japan's Policy Approach
data.description: Japan's former currency chief has expressed concerns that the Bank of Japan is lagging in its policy actions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that he believes raising interest rates could effectively tackle inflation and bring stability to the markets. The former official's remarks underscore the ongoing debate about the central bank's strategy in managing the country's economic challenges.

data.title: STOCKS | MiniMax Group Shares Rise Amid Investor Confidence in AI Technology
data.description: Shares of MiniMax Group experienced a significant increase in Hong Kong, driven by heightened investor confidence in the technology advancements of China's generative AI startups. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing interest in AI innovations as a key factor in the stock's upward movement. This surge reflects the broader optimism surrounding the potential of AI technologies to transform various industries and contribute to economic growth. Investors are closely monitoring developments in the sector, anticipating further advancements and opportunities. The rise in MiniMax Group's shares underscores the market's positive outlook on the capabilities and future prospects of generative AI.

data.title: Oil Market Bears Dominate Current Narrative
data.description: The oil market is currently under the influence of bearish sentiment. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the prevailing narrative is shaped by those expecting a downturn. This perspective is driven by various factors impacting the market dynamics. The current situation reflects a broader trend where bearish views are gaining traction, influencing market participants' expectations and strategies.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Stocks Close Higher on Last Trading Day of the Year of the Snake
data.description: Hong Kong stocks ended higher on the last trading day of the Year of the Snake, with the Hang Seng Index rising by 0.52% and the Hang Seng Tech Index up by 0.13%. According to Jin10, the storage concept and non-ferrous metals sectors showed strength, with Lam Research (06809.HK) closing up 14.05% and Gigadevice Semiconductor rising 9.78%. Additionally, driven by news of its inclusion in the Hang Seng Index, CATL (03750.HK) closed up 2.71%, Lao Feng Xiang (06181.HK) rose 6.64%, and China Molybdenum (03993.HK) increased by 6.35%. Due to the Lunar New Year holiday, the Hong Kong stock market only traded for half a day, closing in the afternoon.

data.title: Brother Machi went long on ETH with 25x leverage and long on HYPE with 10x leverage, and has currently lost over $27.9 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Machi (@machibigbrother) has opened a 10x leveraged long position in HYPE and continues to hold its 25x leveraged long position in ETH. Despite its bullish stance, Machi (@machibigbrother) continues to face losses, with total losses now exceeding $27.9 million.

data.title: A new address spent $2.75 million to buy 21.11 million WLFI tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created address (0xc58...0e6c) spent $2.75 million USDC to buy 21.11 million WLFI tokens at a price of $0.13 per token. This address currently holds $11.21 million USDC and may have used it all for this purchase.
